herb_warnings.explanation: Moringa may lower blood sugar and add to diabetes medication effects.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Monitor blood sugar closely if using moringa.

herb_warnings.explanation: Bitter melon can significantly lower blood sugar.
Garlic may have mild blood sugar lowering effects.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Safe in culinary amounts; monitor if using supplements.

hypertension.display_name: High Blood Pressure / Shinikizo la Damu

herb_warnings.explanation: Garlic can lower blood pressure and may add to medication effects.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Monitor blood pressure; watch for dizziness.
Avoid drinking large amounts if on BP medication.

herb_warnings.explanation: Licorice can raise blood pressure and counteract medications.
Neem may affect liver enzymes and alter malaria drug levels.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Consult doctor before using neem during malaria treatment.

herb_warnings.explanation: Khat can interact with artemether/lumefantrine and may reduce efficacy.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Avoid khat completely during malaria treatment.

herb_warnings.explanation: Khat can reduce the effectiveness of ARVs and cause treatment failure.
Neem may affect liver enzymes and alter ARV levels.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Consult your doctor before using neem.

herb_warnings.explanation: St. John's Wort reduces ARV levels and risks treatment failure.
herb_warnings.recommendation: Do not use with HIV medications.

herb_warnings.explanation: Aloe latex can stimulate uterine contractions and may cause miscarriage.
Neem has been associated with miscarriage.
Culinary amounts of ginger are safe; avoid high-dose supplements.
